title: Cities, Health, and Well-being
catalogDescription: This course analyzes the ways urban built environments bear on physical and mental health and well-being (material-economic resources, security, social relations, open choices). It focuses on how the practices and knowledge of built environment professions and disciplines interact with public health, engineering, and the sciences to understand and change cities. Offered: W.

title: Built Environments Studio
catalogDescription: An intense learning experience in which students are assigned to interdisciplinary, collaborative teams to develop specific approaches to addressing complex issues related to built environments. Focus and format vary with the interdisciplinary team of instructors.
